summary: Matched several lbdvd preload/cache management routines and tightened related lb heap/file prototypes and preload structs. The PR replaced multiple placeholder declarations in src/melee/lb/lbdvd.c with C implementations for heap purge/wait helpers, preload entry allocation, path-to-entry preload registration, persistent heap scene switching, and small spin-wait wrappers. It also adjusted PreloadEntry field signedness, added lbHeap_800158E8/lbHeap_80015900 prototypes, changed lbDvd_803BA68C from a static zero initializer to an extern reset template, and included lb_0192.h for lb_800195D0 wait/yield calls.
what_changed: Implemented previously unmatched lbdvd functions including lbDvd_80017644, lbDvd_80017700, lbDvd_800178E8, lbDvd_80017A80, lbDvd_800189EC, lbDvd_80018C2C, and lbDvd_80018CF4.
Reworked lbDvd_80017598 from a bool cached/preloaded-style parameter to an int heap parameter and made it scan preloadCache.entries for entries on that heap while interrupts are disabled.

Reimplemented lbDvd_80017740 as a preload entry lookup/allocation routine that scans 80 entries, finds a free slot, refreshes load_score for existing matching entries, asserts if no free slot exists, and initializes fields for a new entry.
Added lbDvd_800178E8 to convert a path through lbFile_80016204 and DVDConvertPathToEntrynum before registering the preload request with lbDvd_80017740.
Updated lbDvd_CachePreloadedFile to use clearer indexed access into preloadCache.entries, lbHeap allocation/free functions, lbFile_8001634C/lbFile_800164A4, and void* data handling.

Implemented persistent heap mode switching in lbDvd_80018CF4, including heap persistence toggles through lbHeap_800158D0, waits on lbDvd_80017598 for heaps that are no longer persistent, and final lbHeap_80015900 call.
Adjusted lbDvd_80018C6C switch cases to break rather than return, preserving matched control flow shape.
Changed PreloadEntry fields type, load_state, and entry_num to signed types and changed PreloadCache.persistent_heaps to s32.
Added a TODO noting PreloadCacheScene may need to be smaller based on struct-copy code emitted by functions such as lbDvd_80018CF4.
Updated headers with more specific signatures for lbdvd and lbheap functions, including lbHeap_800158E8 and lbHeap_80015900.
Changed lbDvd_803BA68C in lbdvd.static.h from a static zero-initialized PreloadEntry to an extern PreloadEntry, indicating it is a shared reset/template entry rather than a local definition.
smart_moves: Used OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts around preload cache mutation/scanning in lbDvd_80017598 and lbDvd_80017644, matching the expected atomicity for cache state changes.
Introduced small spin-wait wrappers that call lb_800195D0 while cache/heap cleanup predicates remain active, preserving the original polling behavior without inlining wait logic everywhere.
Added `#pragma dont_inline` around lbDvd_800174E8, suggesting inlining control was needed for a match or for stable call boundaries.
Kept a deliberately shaped `same(int a, int b)` helper and goto-style loop in lbDvd_80017740, likely to reproduce compiler control flow/register allocation for matching.
Converted several ambiguous UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations to concrete prototypes, reducing type drift across lbdvd, lbheap, and lbfile interactions.
Replaced misleading unsigned fields with signed PreloadEntry members where comparisons against negative load_score or signed entry IDs mattered.
Preserved evidence of uncertain struct layout with an explicit TODO rather than overfitting PreloadCacheScene without proof.
decomp_lessons: For lbdvd preload cache work, the entry array size is consistently 80/0x50; loops and struct-copy behavior should be used as evidence for layout and array bounds.
PreloadEntry signedness matters: type, load_state, heap, entry_num, and especially load_score participate in signed comparisons and assignments.
Cache entry invalidation uses a shared zero/reset template entry, lbDvd_803BA68C, copied into entries after freeing archive/raw_data resources.
Heap persistence changes are coupled to waiting for pending preload work to drain; callers should not simply toggle heap flags without polling lbDvd_80017598 and yielding through lb_800195D0.
Header fixes are part of matching: correcting return types and parameter widths can unlock matches in unrelated functions using the same symbols.
When matching MWCC output, unnatural helpers, explicit gotos, and pragma-based inlining control may be acceptable if they preserve codegen.
Do not assume placeholder static initializers are correct; a zero-looking template may need to be extern if the symbol exists in data and is copied by address.
naming_conventions: Functions retain address-based names such as lbDvd_80017740, lbDvd_80018CF4, and lbHeap_800158E8 when semantic names are not yet established.
Struct and cache concepts use CamelCase type names such as PreloadEntry, PreloadCache, PreloadCacheScene, and PreloadCacheSceneEntry.
Global/static data symbols keep address suffixes, e.g. lbDvd_803BA638, lbDvd_803BA68C, lbDvd_804D37F4, and preloadCache.
Field names are descriptive where understood, such as persistent_heaps, persistent_heap, load_state, load_score, entry_num, raw_data, archive, effect_index, and is_heap_persistent.
Temporary match-oriented locals such as var_ctr, var_r22, var_r4, temp_ret, and new_var-style names remain where exact semantics or codegen constraints are unclear.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` around lbDvd_800174E8 to prevent inlining and preserve expected call structure.
Used an inline `same(int a, int b)` helper that returns 0/1 through an explicit local, likely for comparison/codegen shaping in lbDvd_80017740.
Used labels and gotos (`loop_1`, `block_13`) in lbDvd_80017740 to match loop/control-flow output rather than writing idiomatic structured C.
Used explicit constants such as 0x50, 0x2314, 0x270F, 9999, and heap IDs 2, 3, 4, 5 where they appear in preload scoring and heap persistence logic.
Used casts and exact prototype widths, including int versus s16/u8/s8, to influence generated loads/stores and comparisons.
Changed switch branches in lbDvd_80018C6C from early returns to breaks, which can be important for matching branch layout even when behavior is similar.
Introduced static array lbDvd_804D37F4[2] = { 4, 5 } for heap iteration in lbDvd_80018CF4 instead of open-coding both heap IDs.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No PR body or human text excerpt was provided beyond the title, so intent is inferred from the diff and initial classification.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document lbDvd_80017740 and PreloadEntry.effect_index semantics.
Search for follow-up changes to PreloadCacheScene size or the TODO about struct-copy code emitted by lbDvd_80018CF4.
Check whether lbDvd_803BA68C is later defined in another translation unit or converted back after static data matching work.
Look for additional lbdvd matches around lbDvd_80017960, lbDvd_800187F4, and lbDvd_80018A2C.
Audit users of lbDvd_80017598 because the header parameter name remained `preloaded` while the implementation treats it as a heap ID.
